hypothesis: Incorporating humidity-dependent CO2 adsorption capacity as a multi-objective target in the Bayesian optimization framework—using the DFT-derived finding that H2O-amine binding energies systematically exceed CO2-amine binding energies across all amine types—will reveal that polymer-specific pretrained embeddings (PolyNC, polyBERT) maintain their early-discovery advantage over general chemical space embeddings more persistently under competitive water co-adsorption conditions than under dry CO2 conditions, because polymer backbone topology governs steric accessibility to amine sites in ways that monomer-level representations fail to encode.
